title: P3: ProviderLoader registry mapping fn::open to implementations
P2: Integration tests for evaluator Lambda flow
P2: Wire open/session endpoint through EscService → EvaluatorClient → Lambda
description: Full flow: POST .../open → EscService resolves env + imports from DB → EvaluatorClient.evaluate() → Go Lambda → store session in esc_sessions → return session ID. Recursive import resolution before Lambda invoke. Sessions have TTL, encrypted resolved values.
close_reason: openSession/getSession implemented with recursive import resolution (cycle+depth guards), AES-256-GCM encryption via @procella/crypto, session TTL enforcement. 8 new service tests.

title: P2: Implement EvaluatorClient (TS to Go Lambda invocation)
close_reason: LambdaEvaluatorClient using @aws-sdk/client-lambda. Tests mock LambdaClient.send to verify payload shape + error path. Bootstrap picks Lambda vs Unimplemented based on env var.

title: P2: Add SST infra for ESC evaluator Lambda (infra/esc.ts)
close_reason: infra/esc.ts defines ProcellaEscEvaluator function; wired into sst.config.ts; PROCELLA_ESC_EVALUATOR_FN_NAME exposed to CLI API.

title: P2: Implement Go Lambda handler for ESC YAML evaluation
description: Go Lambda accepting {definition, imports, encryptionKey}, calls eval.EvalEnvironment(). EnvironmentLoader reads imports from payload (no network). ProviderLoader stub returns 'unknown provider' for fn::open::*. Built-in functions handled by eval package.
close_reason: Real handler implementing eval.EvalEnvironment with payloadEnvironmentLoader + stubProviderLoader + noopDecrypter. 10 tests cover static values, interpolation, imports, unknown providers, malformed YAML, plus 4 validation + 1 acceptance.

title: P1: Unit tests for EscService + handler tests
close_reason: Covered by: packages/esc/src/service.test.ts (10 integration tests incl. 2 race regressions), packages/esc/src/evaluator-client.test.ts (2 tests), apps/server/src/handlers/esc.test.ts (10 supertest-style handler tests). All 22 ESC tests pass locally + in CI.

title: P1: Wire ESC routes + bootstrap EscService dependency injection
close_reason: Routes + DI wired in cli.ts, index.ts, web.ts, bootstrap.ts. 9 REST routes mounted. PostgresEscService bootstrapped with UnimplementedEvaluatorClient. Handler tests: 10 pass.

title: P1: Create tRPC router for ESC dashboard queries
close_reason: tRPC router for ESC queries at packages/api/src/router/esc.ts — listProjects, listEnvironments, getEnvironment, listRevisions, getRevision

title: P1: Create REST handlers for ESC endpoints (OpenAPI spec)
description: Hono handlers for: POST/GET /api/esc/environments/{org}/{project}, GET/PATCH/DELETE .../{envName}, GET .../{envName}/versions, GET .../{envName}/versions/{version}, POST .../{envName}/open, GET .../{envName}/open/{sessionId}. Match Pulumi ESC OpenAPI spec.
close_reason: REST handlers at apps/server/src/handlers/esc.ts. 9 endpoints matching Pulumi ESC OpenAPI spec. 10 supertest tests covering all handlers + org-mismatch case.

title: P1: Implement EscService class with full CRUD operations
description: Implement EscService class: createEnvironment, listEnvironments, getEnvironment, updateEnvironment, deleteEnvironment (soft), checkExists, cloneEnvironment, listRevisions, createSession, getSession. Follow existing service patterns (constructor DI, error handling).
close_reason: Implemented full CRUD in packages/esc/src/service.ts (commit 40395c2 + race fixes 36c31f6/437b930). 10/10 service.test.ts pass incl. 2 concurrent race regressions. Sessions (openSession/getSession) remain stubs, land in procella-yj7.14.

title: P1: Create Drizzle schema for ESC tables + generate migration
description: Add ESC tables to Drizzle schema: esc_projects (id, org_id, name), esc_environments (id, project_id, name, yaml_body, soft delete), esc_environment_revisions (id, env_id, revision_number, yaml_body, created_by), esc_sessions (id, env_id, revision_id, resolved_values JSONB encrypted, opened_at, expires_at, closed_at). Add unique indexes. Generate migration.
notes:
Session ses_245b1ea89ffeKYBWvv6firvO8J — P1 execution plan:
1. Add 4 tables to packages/db/src/schema.ts: esc_projects, esc_environments, esc_environment_revisions, esc_sessions
2. Column conventions follow existing patterns (uuid PKs, tenant_id text, timestamps, jsonb)
3. Unique indexes: (tenant_id,name) on projects, (project_id,name) on envs, (env_id,revision_number) on revisions
4. Session indexes: (env_id), (expires_at) for GC
5. Export new tables from packages/db/src/index.ts barrel
6. Run 'bun run db:generate' → migration file in packages/db/drizzle/
7. Apply + verify via 'bun run db:migrate' against local PG
8. QA per plan §7.2: psql \dt esc_* shows 4 tables; \di shows unique indexes
close_reason: Superseded. Final migration is 0010_magical_maverick.sql; applies cleanly on fresh PG 17 with resolved_values_ciphertext as text.

title: RBAC & Teams — fine-grained access control
description:
Implement role-based access control with team management.

## What
Fine-grained access control — org roles, team membership, per-stack permissions. Teams can't adopt Procella without per-stack access control.

## Backend Requirements
- `teams` table: name, org_id, description, created_at
- `team_members` junction: team_id, user_id, role (member/admin)
- `team_stacks` table: team_id, stack_id, permission_level (read/write/admin)
- Authorization middleware on ALL routes checking team permissions
- Default roles: org:owner, org:admin, org:member, org:viewer
- Custom roles support (Enterprise)

## API Endpoints
- `POST /api/orgs/{org}/teams` — create team
- `GET /api/orgs/{org}/teams` — list teams
- `GET /api/orgs/{org}/teams/{name}` — get team details
- `PUT /api/orgs/{org}/teams/{name}` — update team
- `DELETE /api/orgs/{org}/teams/{name}` — delete team
- `PUT /api/orgs/{org}/teams/{name}/members/{user}` — add member
- `DELETE /api/orgs/{org}/teams/{name}/members/{user}` — remove member
- `PUT /api/orgs/{org}/teams/{name}/stacks/{stack}` — grant stack access
- `DELETE /api/orgs/{org}/teams/{name}/stacks/{stack}` — revoke stack access

## Pulumi Cloud Tier
Team+ (basic), Enterprise (custom roles)

## Complexity
MEDIUM — touches every route's authorization logic

title: Phase 2: Access Control
description: Enterprise gate. RBAC/Teams for multi-user environments.

title: Organization & Scoped Access Tokens
description:
Implement scoped API tokens for CI/CD pipelines.

## What
Org-level, team-level, and per-stack tokens with expiration. CI/CD pipelines need scoped tokens, not personal tokens.

## Backend Requirements
- `access_tokens` table: name, token_hash, scope (org/team/stack), permissions, expiry, last_used, created_by
- Token validation middleware (extend existing auth system)
- Token rotation support (create new → revoke old)
- Rate limiting per token (optional)

## API Endpoints
- `POST /api/orgs/{org}/tokens` — create scoped token
- `GET /api/orgs/{org}/tokens` — list tokens (redacted)
- `DELETE /api/orgs/{org}/tokens/{id}` — revoke token

## Pulumi Cloud Tier
Team+ (org tokens), Enterprise (team tokens)

## Complexity
LOW-MEDIUM — extends existing auth system
close_reason: Won't implement — Descope access keys cover this natively

title: GitHub App — PR preview comments & commit status checks
description:
Implement GitHub App integration for CI/CD visibility.

## What
GitHub App that posts PR preview comments, sets commit status checks. Phase A (no Deployments needed) — highest-visibility developer experience feature.

## Backend Requirements
- GitHub App registration (client ID, client secret, private key for JWT auth)
- `github_installations` table: org → installation_id, settings, created_at
- GitHub API client (Octokit) for posting comments + status checks
- Hook into update completion events to trigger PR comments
- Webhook receiver endpoint for GitHub events

## API Endpoints
- `POST /api/orgs/{org}/integrations/github` — configure GitHub integration
- `GET /api/orgs/{org}/integrations/github` — get integration status
- `DELETE /api/orgs/{org}/integrations/github` — remove integration
- `POST /api/webhooks/github` — receive GitHub webhook events (installation, PR, push)

## Capabilities (Phase A — no Deployments)
- PR comments showing pulumi preview results (resources added/changed/deleted)
- Commit status checks (pending/success/failure) during updates
- Triggered when CI reports update results back to Procella

## Future (Phase B — requires Deployments)
- Git Push to Deploy: push to main → auto pulumi up
- Review Stacks: ephemeral stack per PR, auto-destroy on merge

## Pulumi Cloud Tier
Free (status checks), Team+ (Review Stacks)

## Complexity
LOW-MEDIUM — GitHub App API well-documented; depends on Webhooks
close_reason: Implemented

title: Stack Search & Filtering — full-text search with tag filtering
description:
Implement full-text search across stacks by name, tags, project, org + advanced filtering.

## What
Unusable at scale without search. S3 backends are 'catastrophically slow with 800+ resources.' PostgreSQL FTS is a quick win.

## Backend Requirements
- PostgreSQL tsvector/GIN index on stacks (name, project, org, tag values)
- Pagination with continuation tokens (not offset-based)
- Tag-based filtering (tagName + tagValue query params)
- Sort by name, last updated, created

## API Endpoints
- `GET /api/user/stacks?organization=&project=&tagName=&tagValue=&query=&continuationToken=`

## Pulumi Cloud Tier
Free (basic), Team+ (advanced)

## Complexity
LOW — PostgreSQL already supports tsvector/GIN indexes
close_reason: Implemented

title: Webhooks — outbound HTTP event delivery
description:
Implement webhook system for stack events (update started/succeeded/failed, drift detected, etc.).

## What
HTTP callbacks on stack events — the #1 integration point for Slack/Teams notifications, CI/CD triggers, and custom workflows.

## Backend Requirements
- `webhooks` table: URL, events filter, secret (HMAC signing), active flag
- `webhook_deliveries` table: payload, HTTP status, response, retry count, timestamps
- HTTP dispatch worker with exponential backoff (max 3 retries)
- HMAC-SHA256 signature in `X-Webhook-Signature` header

## API Endpoints
- `POST /api/orgs/{org}/hooks` — create webhook
- `GET /api/orgs/{org}/hooks` — list webhooks
- `GET /api/orgs/{org}/hooks/{id}` — get webhook details + recent deliveries
- `PUT /api/orgs/{org}/hooks/{id}` — update webhook
- `DELETE /api/orgs/{org}/hooks/{id}` — delete webhook
- `POST /api/orgs/{org}/hooks/{id}/ping` — test webhook

## Event Types
update.started, update.succeeded, update.failed, update.cancelled, stack.created, stack.deleted, drift.detected

## Pulumi Cloud Tier
Team+

## Complexity
LOW — straightforward CRUD + async HTTP dispatch
close_reason: Implemented

title: Audit Logs — append-only user action trail
description:
Implement audit logging for every user/token action.

## What
Append-only log of every user/token action (who did what, when, from where). Enterprise compliance requirement.

## Backend Requirements
- `audit_logs` table: actor (user/token ID), action (enum), resource_type, resource_id, IP address, timestamp, metadata JSONB
- Middleware to capture all mutating API operations automatically
- Export endpoint for compliance (CSV/JSON)
- Retention policy support (configurable TTL)

## API Endpoints
- `GET /api/orgs/{org}/auditlogs?startTime=&endTime=&page=&pageSize=` — query audit logs with pagination
- `GET /api/orgs/{org}/auditlogs/export` — bulk export (CSV or JSON)

## Action Types
stack.create, stack.delete, stack.update, stack.rename, stack.export, stack.import, update.create, update.cancel, user.login, token.create, token.revoke, webhook.create, webhook.delete, settings.update

## Pulumi Cloud Tier
Enterprise+

## Complexity
LOW — middleware + append-only table
close_reason: Implemented

title: Phase 1: Team Essentials
description: Make Procella viable for teams. Includes Webhooks, Audit Logs, Stack Search, GitHub App, and Org/Access Tokens. All low complexity, high community demand features.
close_reason: All Phase 1 features implemented

Create 10+ child subtasks covering: YAML merge engine, string interpolation with JSONPath refs, fn:: builtin registry (fromBase64, fromJSON, toBase64, toJSON, toString, secret, join, etc.), provider interface bridge, diagnostics/trace. Delete esc-eval/ Go module. Remove dependent Go subtasks (.3, .11, .12, .13, .15, .16, .17-.23). Adds ~6-8 weeks to P2. Skip entirely for Path A/B1/B2.

Rewrite Go handler to shell out: 'esc env open --file /tmp/env.yaml'. Benchmark cold-start vs native library path. Skip entirely for Path A/B1/B3.
close_reason: Skipped - Path A chosen (not B2)

Vendor a pinned commit of github.com/pulumi/esc into esc-eval/vendored/ via go.mod replace directive. Document commit hash. Open upstream issue requesting stable API export. Skip entirely for Path A/B2/B3.

description: After procella-yj7.1 validation, record the chosen evaluator path in .sisyphus/analysis/esc-evaluator-decision.md. Options: A) Use github.com/pulumi/esc as Go library directly. B1) Vendor pinned commit. B2) Shell out to esc CLI inside Lambda. B3) TS re-implementation. Must include: importability findings, public API surface, stability assessment, rationale. Blocks P2 and all downstream work.

title: Phase 4: Compliance
description: Enterprise closer. Policy as Code enforcement.

title: Policy as Code (CrossGuard) — resource validation during updates
description:
OPA/Rego or TypeScript policy packs that validate resources during updates.

## What
Compliance automation — 'does this S3 bucket have encryption?' enforced at deploy time. Enterprise non-negotiable.

## Backend Requirements
- `policy_packs` table: name, org_id, version, runtime (opa/typescript), blob_ref
- `policy_groups` table: name, org_id, stacks filter (all/tagged/specific)
- `policy_pack_versions` table: pack_id, version, schema, blob_ref
- `policy_results` table: update_id, pack_id, outcome (mandatory/advisory), violations JSONB
- Blob storage for policy pack content (OPA bundles or JS bundles)
- 2-phase upload: create metadata → upload content
- Policy evaluation during update lifecycle (after checkpoint, before complete)

## API Endpoints
- `POST /api/orgs/{org}/policypacks` — create policy pack (returns upload URL)
- `PUT /api/orgs/{org}/policypacks/{name}/versions/{version}` — upload pack content
- `GET /api/orgs/{org}/policypacks` — list policy packs
- `POST /api/orgs/{org}/policygroups` — create policy group (link packs to stacks)
- `GET /api/orgs/{org}/policygroups` — list policy groups
- `GET /api/stacks/{org}/{project}/{stack}/update/{id}/policy-results` — get results

## Pulumi Cloud Tier
Enterprise+

## Complexity
MEDIUM — policy evaluation engine is the hard part. Can start with OPA sidecar integration.

title: Drift Detection — detect out-of-band infrastructure changes
description:
Scheduled pulumi refresh --preview-only to detect configuration drift.

## What
April 2024 launch — Pulumi's 'top customer ask.' Detects when someone modifies infrastructure outside of Pulumi (console clicks, scripts, etc.).

## Backend Requirements
- Depends on Scheduled Operations
- `drift_results` table: stack_id, schedule_id, detected_at, diff (JSONB), acknowledged, acknowledged_by
- Runs `pulumi refresh --preview-only` on schedule
- Stores resource diff when changes detected
- Fires webhook (drift.detected event) for notifications
- Dashboard UI to view drift status per stack

## API Endpoints
- `POST /api/stacks/{org}/{project}/{stack}/deployments/drift/schedule` — configure drift schedule
- `GET /api/stacks/{org}/{project}/{stack}/drift` — get latest drift status
- `GET /api/stacks/{org}/{project}/{stack}/drift/history` — drift detection history
- `POST /api/stacks/{org}/{project}/{stack}/drift/{id}/acknowledge` — acknowledge drift

## Pulumi Cloud Tier
Enterprise+

## Complexity
MEDIUM — requires Scheduled Operations + preview execution
Can implement initially as 'suggested cron' that users run externally + report endpoint

title: TTL Stacks — auto-destroy ephemeral environments
description:
Auto-destroy stacks after a configurable duration.

## What
CEO called it 'fan favorite.' Prevents cloud cost leaks from forgotten stacks. Perfect for preview/staging environments.

## Backend Requirements
- `ttl` column on stacks table (nullable timestamp or duration)
- `ttl_schedules` table: stack_id, destroy_at, warned_at, status
- Background worker to check expiration (runs every minute)
- Warning notification before destruction (configurable, e.g., 1hr before)
- Without Deployments: fires webhook for external runner to destroy
- With Deployments: triggers server-side destroy directly

## API Endpoints
- `POST /api/stacks/{org}/{project}/{stack}/deployments/ttl/schedule` — set TTL
- `GET /api/stacks/{org}/{project}/{stack}/deployments/ttl` — get TTL status
- `DELETE /api/stacks/{org}/{project}/{stack}/deployments/ttl` — remove TTL
- `POST /api/stacks/{org}/{project}/{stack}/deployments/ttl/extend` — extend TTL

## Pulumi Cloud Tier
Enterprise+

## Complexity
MEDIUM — needs either Deployments or external trigger mechanism
Alternative: 'mark for destruction' + webhook to external runner

title: Scheduled Operations — cron-based Pulumi operations
description:
Run any Pulumi operation on a cron schedule.

## What
Foundation for Drift Detection and TTL. Enables GitOps-style reconciliation loops.

## Backend Requirements
- `schedules` table: stack_id, cron_expression, operation_type (up/refresh/destroy/preview), enabled, last_run, next_run, created_by
- Cron evaluation worker (runs every minute, checks next_run)
- Without Deployments: fires webhooks for external runners to execute
- With Deployments: triggers server-side execution directly

## API Endpoints
- `POST /api/stacks/{org}/{project}/{stack}/deployments/schedules` — create schedule
- `GET /api/stacks/{org}/{project}/{stack}/deployments/schedules` — list schedules
- `PUT /api/stacks/{org}/{project}/{stack}/deployments/schedules/{id}` — update schedule
- `DELETE /api/stacks/{org}/{project}/{stack}/deployments/schedules/{id}` — delete schedule
- `POST /api/stacks/{org}/{project}/{stack}/deployments/schedules/{id}/pause` — pause/resume

## Pulumi Cloud Tier
Enterprise+

## Complexity
MEDIUM — cron evaluation + operation triggering

title: Phase 3: Operations
description: Infrastructure platform features. Scheduled Operations, Drift Detection, TTL Stacks.

title: Consolidate pgErrorCode helper into @procella/db (duplicated in stacks + esc)
description: pgErrorCode helper is now duplicated in packages/stacks/src/index.ts and packages/esc/src/service.ts. Move to packages/db/src/index.ts and import from there. Small refactor; file-local copies should still typecheck during migration.

title: docker-compose: postgres:18-alpine fails with /var/lib/postgresql/data mount
description: docker-compose.yml uses postgres:18-alpine but the 18+ image requires mount at /var/lib/postgresql (not /var/lib/postgresql/data). Current config fails to start with 'unused mount/volume' error. Fix: pin to postgres:17-alpine (matches project spec per AGENTS.md 'PostgreSQL 17') OR update mount path. Affects 'bun run dev' and 'bun run dev:deps'. Found during procella-yj7.5 work; worked around with standalone pg17-esc container.

title: Drizzle snapshot drift: oidc_trust_policies indexes (fixed in procella-yj7.5 migration)
description:
Migration 0009_oidc_ci_auth.sql did not regenerate snapshot for oidc_trust_policies index changes in schema.ts. Caused 0009_snapshot.json to lag schema.ts. Detected when generating 0008_nostalgic_avengers.sql (procella-yj7.5) which included DROP IF EXISTS + re-create of oidc_trust_org_name and add idx_oidc_trust_org_issuer.

Fixed incidentally in procella-yj7.5 migration. No action needed unless future oidc schema changes reveal further drift. If they do, run 'bun run db:generate' on a fresh checkout with PROCELLA_DATABASE_URL set to detect staleness early.

title: P6: OpenTelemetry spans for ESC operations
close_reason: OTel spans added to EscService write ops + openSession nested spans (resolveImports, evaluator.invoke, session.store) + gcSweep. Read-only paths not instrumented (hot paths).

title: P6: Session GC — add expired session cleanup to cron Lambda
close_reason: escGcSweep added to gc-bootstrap cron; pg_try_advisory_lock(0x455343475f455343) for cluster safety; soft-close with LIMIT 1000. Test asserts expired+open sessions get closed while others are untouched.

title: P5: Session management UI (active sessions, expiry)
P4: Drafts/change requests for environment changes
P4: Environment tags CRUD (metadata key-value pairs)
P4: Revision tags CRUD (stable, production, etc.)
SAML SSO & SCIM provisioning
description:
Enterprise SSO via SAML 2.0 and automatic user provisioning via SCIM.

## What
Enterprise compliance requirement. Non-negotiable for large organizations using identity providers like Okta, Azure AD, OneLogin.

## Backend Requirements
- SAML 2.0 Service Provider implementation (or leverage Descope's SAML support)
- IdP metadata storage and configuration
- SCIM 2.0 endpoints for automatic user and group provisioning
- JIT (Just-in-Time) user creation on first SAML login
- Group-to-team mapping

## API Endpoints
- `GET /api/orgs/{org}/saml/sso` — get SAML config
- `PUT /api/orgs/{org}/saml/sso` — configure SAML
- `GET /api/orgs/{org}/saml/metadata` — SP metadata XML
- `POST /api/orgs/{org}/saml/acs` — Assertion Consumer Service
- `GET/POST /api/scim/v2/Users` — SCIM user operations
- `GET/POST /api/scim/v2/Groups` — SCIM group operations

## Notes
Descope already supports SAML/SSO — may only need configuration UI + SCIM layer on top.

## Pulumi Cloud Tier
Enterprise (SAML), Business Critical (SCIM)

## Complexity
MEDIUM (SAML via Descope) / HIGH (full SCIM)

title: Pulumi Insights — resource search & cloud inventory
description:
Search and inventory across ALL resources in ALL stacks.

## What
'Find all S3 buckets without encryption' across your entire infrastructure. Cloud asset inventory without additional tooling.

## Backend Requirements
- Resource indexing pipeline: parse checkpoints → extract individual resources → store in searchable index
- `resources` table: urn, type, package, name, stack_id, properties JSONB, provider, parent_urn
- Full-text search on resource properties
- Aggregation queries (count by type, by provider, by stack)
- Incremental indexing: update resource index on each checkpoint update

## API Endpoints
- `GET /api/orgs/{org}/search/resources?query=&type=&package=&stack=` — search resources
- `GET /api/orgs/{org}/insights/summary` — resource counts by type/provider
- `GET /api/orgs/{org}/insights/resources/{urn}` — resource details + history

## Pulumi Cloud Tier
Team+ (basic), Enterprise+ (AI-powered)

## Complexity
HIGH — checkpoint parsing + resource indexing pipeline + query engine

title: Pulumi ESC — Environments, Secrets & Config management
description:
Centralized hierarchical config/secrets management.

## What
Separate Pulumi product (GA 2024). Solves 'secrets sprawl' across stacks. YAML-based environment definitions with composition and dynamic credentials.

## Backend Requirements
- `environments` table: name, org_id, yaml_content, created_by
- `environment_versions` table: env_id, version, yaml_content, created_at
- YAML composition engine (environment inheritance/imports)
- Static values: straightforward key-value with interpolation
- Dynamic credentials (Phase 2): OIDC token exchange with cloud providers (AWS, GCP, Azure)
- Value resolution: open environment → merge hierarchy → resolve interpolations → return values

## API Endpoints
- `POST /api/orgs/{org}/environments` — create environment
- `GET /api/orgs/{org}/environments` — list environments
- `GET /api/orgs/{org}/environments/{name}` — get environment definition
- `PUT /api/orgs/{org}/environments/{name}` — update environment
- `DELETE /api/orgs/{org}/environments/{name}` — delete environment
- `POST /api/orgs/{org}/environments/{name}/open` — resolve and return values
- `GET /api/orgs/{org}/environments/{name}/versions` — version history

## Pulumi Cloud Tier
Free (limited), Team+ (full)

## Complexity
HIGH — static config is medium, dynamic OIDC credentials are hard. Recommend implementing static-only first.

title: Phase 5: Platform
description: Long-term vision. Deployments, ESC, Insights, SSO, AI. High complexity features.

title: Pulumi Deployments — server-side remote execution
description:
Server-side pulumi up/preview/destroy — the equivalent of Terraform Cloud runs.

## What
Removes need for CI/CD pipelines. Git Push to Deploy, Review Stacks, Click to Deploy from dashboard.

## Backend Requirements
- Job queue (PostgreSQL-based or Redis): deployment_jobs table with status, priority, assigned_worker
- Isolated execution environments: Docker containers with Pulumi CLI + providers
- Git checkout: clone repo, checkout branch/commit, detect Pulumi project
- Log streaming: real-time stdout/stderr via SSE or WebSocket
- `deployment_settings` table: stack_id, source (git URL, branch), env vars, pre/post commands
- `deployments` table: id, stack_id, trigger (push/manual/schedule), status, logs_ref, started_at, completed_at
- Worker pool management (horizontal scaling)
- Source control integration (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket)

## API Endpoints
- `POST /api/stacks/{org}/{project}/{stack}/deployments` — trigger deployment
- `GET /api/stacks/{org}/{project}/{stack}/deployments` — list deployments
- `GET /api/stacks/{org}/{project}/{stack}/deployments/{id}` — get deployment status
- `GET /api/stacks/{org}/{project}/{stack}/deployments/{id}/logs` — stream logs (SSE)
- `POST /api/stacks/{org}/{project}/{stack}/deployments/{id}/cancel` — cancel deployment
- `PUT /api/stacks/{org}/{project}/{stack}/deployment-settings` — configure deployment source

## Unlocks
- GitHub App Phase B (Git Push to Deploy, Review Stacks)
- Drift Detection (server-side preview)
- TTL Stacks (server-side destroy)

## Pulumi Cloud Tier
Free (limited), Team+ (full)

## Complexity
HIGH — entire execution substrate (containers, git, log streaming, queuing). Essentially building a mini CI/CD system.

title: Cross-cutting: per-tenant CMK/KMS encryption (BYOK)
description: Current encryption uses a single PROCELLA_ENCRYPTION_KEY master with HKDF per-env derivation. BYOK support (AWS KMS CMK per tenant) would allow regulated customers to manage their own root keys. Cross-cutting — affects stacks, esc, webhooks secrets, etc. Not ESC-specific.

title: Cross-cutting: ticket-ID/justification required on privileged mutations (SOC2 audit)
description: Enterprise audit requirement: all privileged mutations (stacks, webhooks, esc, oidc, github app) should require a ticketId + justification field, persisted to audit log for SOC2/SOX compliance. Cross-cutting refactor — touches every mutation handler. Not ESC-specific; flagged by cubic during procella-yj7 review.

title: AI Copilot — natural language infrastructure queries
description:
Natural language queries over infrastructure state.

## What
'Show me all Lambda functions using Python 3.8' — query infrastructure using natural language. Requires Insights (#13) as foundation.

## Backend Requirements
- Depends on Pulumi Insights (resource index)
- LLM integration (OpenAI/Anthropic API)
- Query translation: natural language → structured resource query
- Response formatting: resource list with relevant properties
- Chat interface in dashboard

## Pulumi Cloud Tier
Enterprise+

## Complexity
HIGH — depends on Insights + LLM integration. Lowest priority.

value:
OIDC CI Debugging Lessons (April 2026)

## Lesson 1: Diagnose Before Acting — Always Check What the CLI Sees

When debugging Pulumi CLI failures against the Procella backend:
1. FIRST run `pulumi whoami -v` to see what org/user the CLI sees
2. FIRST run `pulumi org get-default` to see the default org
3. FIRST check the /api/user response format matches what the CLI expects
4. ONLY THEN attempt fixes

The CLI silently exits 0 when it can't resolve an org. No error message, no output.
This wasted 5+ commits of trial-and-error with different stack name formats.

## Lesson 2: Pulumi CLI Silent Failures

Pulumi CLI v3.229.0 has multiple silent failure modes against service backends:
- `stack init` exits 0 without creating/selecting if the org doesn't match
- `stack select --create` exits 0 without creating/selecting for the same reason
- No error message on stdout OR stderr — only detectable by checking `pulumi stack --show-name` afterward

Workaround: Create stacks via direct API calls (curl), then use `pulumi stack select` for CLI operations.

## Lesson 3: The /api/user/organizations/default Endpoint

The Pulumi CLI calls GET /api/user/organizations/default to resolve the default org for stack operations.
If this endpoint doesn't return the correct org, the CLI silently fails on ALL stack operations.
In Hono, a parameterized route like /user/organizations/:orgName will catch 'default' as an orgName.
Handle orgName==='default' explicitly inside the handler.

## Lesson 4: Descope Tenant Name vs Trust Policy orgSlug

The Descope tenant name in JWT claims may differ from the orgSlug used in trust policies.
When the OIDC exchange mints an access key, the JWT's tenant name comes from Descope,
but the trust policy's orgSlug comes from the policy creator. These can diverge.
Solution: Store the orgSlug as an explicit custom claim (OidcClaims.orgSlug) in the minted access key,
and prefer it in extractOrgSlug() over the Descope-derived tenant name.

## Lesson 5: SST Concurrency Group Isolation

preview.yml and preview-cleanup.yml MUST use different concurrency groups.
If they share the same group with cancel-in-progress: true, closing then reopening a PR
causes the deploy to cancel the running sst remove, leaving CloudFront half-destroyed.
The health check + drift recovery pattern (sst unlock → remove → redeploy) handles this gracefully.

## Lesson 6: Don't Shotgun Debug — Ask 'What Does the Client See?'

When a CLI tool fails silently against your API:
1. Check what the tool's auth/identity endpoints return (not just healthz)
2. Compare response format against the upstream source (Go types, OpenAPI spec)
3. Add the tool's verbose/debug flags FIRST, not as a last resort
4. Test API endpoints directly with curl before blaming the CLI

This applies to any CLI-to-API debugging, not just Pulumi.
